COURT MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PRE-JUDGMENT RECEIVER, JOHN HAWKINS MOTION FOR: (1) APPROVAL OF PRE-JUDGMENT RECEIVERS FINAL ACCOUNT AND REPORT; (2) APPROVAL OF FINAL COMPENSATION AND REIMUBURSEMENT OF COSTS FOR PRE-JUDGMENT RECEIVER AND HIS ATTORNEYS AND OTHER PROFESSIONALS; (3) DISCHARGE OF PRE-JUDGMENT RECEIVER; AND (4) EXONERATION OF PRE-JUDGMENT RECEIVERS BOND IS CONTINUED TO MAY 24, 2017, AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302, ONLY ON THE ISSUES OF THE PROPRIETY OF MR. HAWKINS' APPROVAL OF THE OFFICE LEASE RENEWAL AND WHETHER MR. HAWKINS AND HIS COUNSEL SHOULD BE REIMBURSED FOR THEIR FEES AND COSTS INCURRED IN OPPOSING UNION BANK'S CHALLENGE TO THIS MOTION. THE PARTIES AGREE TO THE FOLLOWING BRIEFING SCHEDULE: ANY FURTHER OBJECTIONS OR RESPONSES TO PRE-JUDGMENT RECEIVER'S SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSE REGARDING PRE-JUDGMENT RECEIVER'S FINAL ACCOUNT AND REPORT SHALL BE FILED AND SERVED BY MAY 3, 2017; AND FURTHER REPLY OR RESPONSE BY THE PRE-JUDGMENT RECEIVER SHALL BE FILED AND SERVED BY MAY 17, 2017. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For: (1) Approval Of Pre-Judgment Receivers Final Account And Report; (2) Approval Of Final Compensation And Reimubursement Of Costs For Pre-Judgment Receiver And His Attorneys And Other Professionals; (3) Discharge Of Pre-Judgment Receiver; And (4) Exoneration Of Pre-Judgment Receivers Bond
Off Calendar May-24-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Pre-Judgment Receiver John Hawkins' motion for approval of his final account and report and for other relief including payment of his and his counsel's fees and costs in responding to the objections of plaintiff MUFG Union Bank, N.A.'s objections to this motion is granted in its entirety. Mr. Hawkins is granted all the relief he sought in this motion and he and his counsel are awarded fees of $4,647 and 29,722 respectively for their work in responding to Union Bank's objections. Union Bank has not shown that any of its objections to this motion have merit. In particular, Union Bank has not shown that Mr. Hawkins breached any duty or otherwise acted improperly by approving the lease renewal. Mr. Hawkins had the authority to approve the lease renewal and his doing so was reasonable and proper. Mr. Hawkins and his counsel are entitled to an award of fees and costs for their work in opposing Union Bank's objections. The amounts sought are reasonable and were reasonably incurred. Union Bank has not provided any bases why there should be a further continuance of this motion. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189; Dept. 302.
